scenarios.behavior: Honor one canonical Range request with 206, Content-Range, identity encoding, exact length, and the pinned strong ETag.
Ignore the first Range request and return one bounded identity-encoded 200 full entity.
Return a valid initial 206 without a strong ETag, causing exactly one range-free bounded full restart.
Return a syntactically valid weak ETag on the initial range, which is not usable for entity pinning.
Pin the reference strong ETag, then return a different strong ETag for a payload request.
Return a 206 whose Content-Range total differs from the declared canonical file length.
End an otherwise valid response one byte before its exact declared range length.
Emit one byte beyond the exact active response-body cap.
Return Content-Encoding gzip for bytes that would otherwise match the fixture.
Emit a valid first chunk, then leave the body pending beyond the idle watchdog.
Flip one deterministic byte inside the first selected-rendition unit while retaining all HTTP metadata.
Flip one deterministic byte inside the initial static PNG while retaining all HTTP metadata.
Change the first normally requested selected-rendition or static alignment-padding byte from zero to one.
Return exactly one range-free bounded 200 whose whole-file digest matches provenance.
Return exactly one range-free bounded 200 while the host supplies a mismatching whole-file digest.
